hideUnchanged.title: Hide Unchanged Cells
hideUnchanged.description: Whether unchanged cells should be hidden by default.

autolaunch.title: Whether to autorun slideshow upon opening
autolaunch.description: Autorun slideshow upon opening if set; active only on notebooks that have a 'livereveal' or 'rise' section in their metadata; this provision is here to avoid starting RISE on regular notebooks that are not intended for slideshow.

enable_chalkboard.title: Whether to activate chalkboard

start_slideshow_at.title: Where to start the slideshow
start_slideshow_at.description: A string that describes where to start the slideshow this can be either `beginning` (start on first slide) or `selected` (start on the slide that contains the currently selected cell)

auto_select.title: What to select when new content appears
auto_select.description: a string that specifies how to select cells when new contents is displayed during the slideshow (typically, new slide or new fragment). It can be either `none` (no automatic selection), or `code` (select first code cell in new content).

header.title: HTML Header
header.description: the html text for a header that will show up on all slides. (effective only if `overlay` is not defined)

footer.title: HTML Footer
footer.description: the html text for a footer that will show up on all slides. (effective only if `overlay` is not defined)

backimage.title: Background image
backimage.description: a (relative) filename for a background image that will show up on all slides. (effective only if `overlay` is not defined)

overlay.title: HTML Overlay
overlay.description: some html text that will be inserted as-is on all pages. If this is provided, the 3 previous settings (header, footer and backimage) are ignored altogether.

theme.title: Reveal Theme
theme.description:
This setting, like the ones below, is passed directly to [`reveal.js` see this doc](https://github.com/hakimel/reveal.js).
theme: [see a list of reveal's available native themes](https://github.com/hakimel/reveal.js#theming). Note that dark themes from reveal do not work well with RISE, at least not without additional and manual tweaks.

transition.title: Reveal Transition
transition.description: at least available: none - fade - slide - convex - concave - zoom - cube - linear; see also `reveal.js`.

slideNumber.title: Reveal Slide Number
slideNumber.description: A string to specify if and how to number slides; [see reveal's explanations](https://github.com/hakimel/reveal.js#slide-number).

width.title: Reveal Width
width.description: A css width attribute, like `1920px` or `10cm` or `100%`. **Remember that you can always zoom in and out** with you browser's native keyboard shortcuts like typically `Command +` or `Command -`.

controls.title: Reveal Controls
controls.description: Add buttons to exit and get help

progress.title: Reveal Progress
progress.description: Show progress as a line at the bottom

history.title: Reveal History
history.description: Push each slide change to the browser history

auto_select_timeout.title: Timeout before auto-selection
auto_select_timeout.description: timeout in milliseconds before auto-selecting; all the timeout settings are mostly intended for developers.

restore_timeout.title: Timeout when restoring the focus
restore_timeout.description: timeout in milliseconds before restoring focus to selected cell when quitting slideshow mode; mostly intended for developers.

async_timeout.title: Timeout between synchronization
async_timeout.description: timeout in milliseconds before an extra call to Reveal.sync() is made to ensure proper display in some problematic conditions; like other timeout settings this is mostly targetting developers.

sendWithShiftEnter.description: Whether to send a message via Shift-Enter instead of Enter.

stackMessages.description: Whether to stack consecutive messages from same user.

unreadNotifications.description: Whether to enable or not the notifications on unread messages.

enableCodeToolbar.description: Whether to enable or not the code toolbar.

defaultDirectory.description: Default directory where to create and look for chat, the jupyter root directory if empty.

toolbar.title: File browser toolbar items

disabled.title: Whether the item is ignored or not

icon.title: Item icon id
icon.description: If defined, it will override the command icon

label.title: Item label
label.description: If defined, it will override the command label

caption.title: Item caption
caption.description: If defined, it will override the command caption

saveState.title: Save Jupyter widget state in notebooks
saveState.description: Automatically save Jupyter widget state when a notebook is saved.

local_config.title: Allow local nbgrader config file
local_config.description:
This setting allows the use of a local config file for formgrader.
WARNING: using the local configuration file has consequences for the server, it should not be used if several users are using the same instance of Jupyterlab.

blockWhileCommandExecutes.title: Suspend user interaction until commands finish
blockWhileCommandExecutes.description: Suspend JupyterLab user interaction until Git commands (e.g., commit, pull, reset, revert) finish executing. Setting this to true helps mitigate potential race conditions leading to data loss, conflicts, and a broken Git history. Unless running a slow network, UI suspension should not interfere with standard workflows. Setting this to false allows for actions to trigger multiple concurrent Git actions.

cancelPullMergeConflict.title: Cancel pull merge conflict
cancelPullMergeConflict.description: If true, when fetching and integrating changes from a remote repository, a conflicting merge is canceled and the working tree left untouched.

disableBranchWithChanges.title: Disable branch with changes
disableBranchWithChanges.description: Disable all branch operations (new, switch) when there are changed/staged files

displayStatus.title: Display Git status updates
displayStatus.description: Display Git extension status updates in the JupyterLab status bar. If true, the extension displays status updates in the JupyterLab status bar, such as when pulling and pushing changes, switching branches, and polling for changes. Depending on the level of extension activity, some users may find the status updates distracting. In which case, setting this to false should reduce visual noise.

doubleClickDiff.title: Show diff on double click
doubleClickDiff.description: If true, double clicking a file in the list of changed files will open a diff.

historyCount.title: History count
historyCount.description: Number of (most recent) commits shown in the history log

refreshIfHidden.title: Refresh if the Git tab is hidden
refreshIfHidden.description: Whether to check Git status when the Git tab is not visible. Choose `false` for higher performance.

refreshInterval.title: Refresh interval
refreshInterval.description: Number of milliseconds between polling the file system for changes.

simpleStaging.title: Simple staging flag
simpleStaging.description: If true, use a simplified concept of staging. Only files with changes are shown (instead of showing staged/changed/untracked), and all files with changes will be automatically staged

commitAndPush.title: Trigger push on commit
commitAndPush.description: Whether to trigger or not a push for each commit.

promptUserIdentity.title: Prompt user identity on commit
promptUserIdentity.description: Whether to prompt for user name and email for each commit.

openFilesBehindWarning.title: Open files behind warning
openFilesBehindWarning.description: If true, a popup dialog will be displayed if a user opens a file that is behind its remote branch version, or if an opened file has updates on the remote branch.

hideHiddenFileWarning.title: Hide hidden file warning
hideHiddenFileWarning.description: If true, the warning popup when opening the .gitignore file without hidden files will not be displayed.

clearOutputsBeforeCommit.title: Clear outputs before commit
clearOutputsBeforeCommit.description: If true, notebook outputs will be cleared before committing. If false, outputs are kept. If null, ask each time.
